questions.q: Which space agency carried out the Chandrayaan-3 mission?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 was developed and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: In which year did Chandrayaan-3 successfully land on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3's lander touched down on the Moon on 23 August 2023.

questions.q: Near which region of the Moon did Chandrayaan-3 make its historic landing?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 landed near the lunar south pole, the first mission ever to do so.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-3 made India the first country to land near the Moon's south pole. Which milestone did it achieve for soft-landing on the Moon overall?
questions.explanation: India became the fourth nation to achieve a soft landing on the Moon, after the USSR, the USA and China.

questions.q: What was the name of the Chandrayaan-3 lander?
questions.explanation: The lander was named Vikram, after Vikram Sarabhai, the founder of the Indian space programme.

questions.q: What was the name of the rover deployed by Chandrayaan-3?
questions.explanation: The six-wheeled rover was named Pragyan, a Sanskrit word meaning 'wisdom'.

questions.q: After whom was the Chandrayaan-3 lander 'Vikram' named?
questions.explanation: The lander honoured Vikram Sarabhai, widely regarded as the father of the Indian space programme.

questions.q: What does the word 'Chandrayaan' broadly mean?
questions.explanation: 'Chandrayaan' translates roughly to 'Moon craft' or 'Moon vehicle' in Sanskrit and Hindi.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-3 was launched from which Indian spaceport?
questions.explanation: The mission lifted off from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre on Sriharikota island.

questions.q: Which earlier Indian mission was Chandrayaan-3 a follow-up to, aiming to complete its soft-landing goal?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 was a successor to Chandrayaan-2, whose lander had crashed in 2019.

questions.q: On what date did the Vikram lander touch down on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Vikram soft-landed on 23 August 2023, a date India later declared as National Space Day.

questions.q: Which launch vehicle carried Chandrayaan-3 into space?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 was launched on the LVM3 (also known as GSLV Mk III), ISRO's heaviest launcher.

questions.q: On what date was Chandrayaan-3 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 was launched on 14 July 2023 and reached the Moon about six weeks later.

questions.q: Unlike Chandrayaan-2, Chandrayaan-3 did NOT include which component?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 carried a propulsion module instead of a full science orbiter, since Chandrayaan-2's orbiter was still functioning.

questions.q: What name did India give to the landing site of Chandrayaan-3 on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the landing site would be called 'Shiv Shakti' Point.

questions.q: Following the landing, India designated 23 August as which national observance?
questions.explanation: The government declared 23 August as National Space Day to mark the Chandrayaan-3 landing.

questions.q: Who was the Chairman of ISRO who led the organisation during the Chandrayaan-3 landing?
questions.explanation: S. Somanath was the ISRO Chairman at the time of Chandrayaan-3's successful landing.

questions.q: Roughly how long was the planned surface operating duration of the Vikram lander and Pragyan rover?
options: About one lunar day (around 14 Earth days)
questions.explanation: The lander and rover were designed to operate for one lunar daytime, roughly 14 Earth days, before lunar night.

questions.q: Which instrument on the Pragyan rover analysed the chemical composition of the lunar soil?
questions.explanation: The Alpha Particle X-ray Spectrometer (APXS) on Pragyan measured the elemental composition of the lunar surface.

questions.q: The ChaSTE instrument aboard Vikram measured what property of the Moon's surface?
questions.explanation: ChaSTE (Chandra's Surface Thermophysical Experiment) probed the near-surface temperature and thermal conductivity of the lunar soil.

questions.q: Which element's presence near the south pole was confirmed in situ by Chandrayaan-3, alongside others?
questions.explanation: The APXS and LIBS instruments confirmed sulphur in the lunar soil near the south pole, along with aluminium, iron and other elements.

questions.q: What was the approximate launch mass of the Chandrayaan-3 spacecraft (propulsion module plus lander and rover)?
questions.explanation: The total Chandrayaan-3 stack had a launch mass of roughly 3,900 kilograms.

questions.q: What was the chief reason given for the failure of Chandrayaan-2's lander that Chandrayaan-3 was designed to overcome?
options: A software glitch and thrust anomaly during the final descent
A solar panel failure in orbit
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2's Vikram crashed due to anomalies during powered descent, so Chandrayaan-3 was built with more robust descent guidance and software.

questions.q: Which LIBS-based instrument on the Pragyan rover detected sulphur and other elements using laser pulses?
questions.explanation: The Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscope (LIBS) on Pragyan vaporised soil with laser pulses to identify its elemental makeup.

questions.q: The ILSA instrument carried on the Vikram lander was designed to record what?
questions.explanation: ILSA (Instrument for Lunar Seismic Activity) was meant to detect seismic events near the landing site.

questions.q: The Vikram lander carried a NASA-provided passive instrument for precise distance measurement. What was it?
questions.explanation: NASA contributed a Laser Retroreflector Array (LRA) to Vikram for long-term lunar ranging studies.

questions.q: After landing, Vikram performed an unplanned demonstration manoeuvre on the Moon. What was it?
options: A short drive of the lander
A 'hop' experiment, firing engines to lift and relocate
questions.explanation: ISRO conducted a successful 'hop' experiment in which Vikram fired its engines to rise and set down a short distance away.

questions.q: The Chandrayaan-3 propulsion module carried a science payload to study Earth from lunar orbit. What was it called?
questions.explanation: SHAPE (Spectro-polarimetry of HAbitable Planet Earth) studied Earth's spectral signature as a template for finding exoplanets.

questions.q: Why was Chandrayaan-3 unable to re-establish contact with Vikram and Pragyan after the first lunar night?
options: Their electronics likely could not survive the extreme cold of the lunar night
They ran out of rocket fuel
They were buried by a dust storm
questions.explanation: The lander and rover were not designed to endure the roughly minus-200 C lunar night, and did not wake up afterward.

questions.q: Approximately how many days after launch did Chandrayaan-3 achieve its lunar soft landing?
questions.explanation: Launched on 14 July and landing on 23 August 2023, the journey took roughly 40 days using a fuel-efficient phased trajectory.
